title: [$250] Pending card transaction detail view briefly shows then goes blank with Join button

labels.name: External
labels.description: Added to denote the issue can be worked on by a contributor

labels.name: Daily
labels.description: KSv2

labels.name: Help Wanted
labels.description: Apply this label when an issue is open to proposals by contributors

labels.name: Bug
labels.description: Something is broken. Auto assigns a BugZero manager.

labels.name: Hot Pick
labels.description: Ready for an engineer to pick up and run with

body:
## Description When clicking on a pending Expensify Card transaction to view its details, the transaction detail view (RHP) briefly renders the correct content (receipt image, amount, merchant, date, category, card info) and then immediately goes blank. The header changes to show a "Join" button, and the content area becomes empty. This has been reported on staging at `staging.new.expensify.com`. ## Steps to Reproduce 1. Open an expense report that contains pending Expensify Card transactions 2. Click on one of the pending card transactions to view its details 3. Observe the transaction details briefly appear then disappear ## Expected Behavior The transaction detail view should remain visible and interactive, showing the receipt, amount, merchant, description, date, category, card, and attendees. ## Actual Behavior - Transaction details flash briefly (less than 1 second) then disappear - An empty page is shown with only a "Join" button in the header - The network tab shows many repeated `Graphite` API calls - `AuthenticatePusher` returns a 403 error in the console - The console shows the `FullPageNotFoundView` is being triggered ## Technical Analysis The likely root cause is that `isParentActionMissingAfterLoad` evaluates to `true` for pending card transaction threads. When the parent report actions are loaded (via `OpenReport`), the IOU action that corresponds to the pending transaction is either not present or gets filtered out. This causes `isDeletedTransactionThread` to become `true` in `ReportScreen`, which triggers `shouldShowNotFoundPage`, clearing the view. The "Join" button appears because transaction thread reports have `notificationPreference: HIDDEN`, and `canJoinChat()` returns `true` for hidden chat threads. When the header falls back from `MoneyRequestHeader` to `HeaderView`, the Join button is rendered. The excessive Graphite calls are a side effect: each render of `FullPageNotFoundView` with `shouldShow=true` calls `StatsCounter('FullPageNotFoundView')`, firing a Graphite API request. ### Key files involved - `src/pages/inbox/ReportScreen.tsx` — `isDeletedTransactionThread`, `shouldShowNotFoundPage`, header selection - `src/libs/TransactionNavigationUtils.ts` — `getParentReportActionDeletionStatus` and `isParentActionMissingAfterLoad` - `src/pages/inbox/HeaderView.tsx` — `canJoin` / Join button display - `src/libs/ReportUtils.ts` — `canJoinChat` returning true for hidden transaction threads - `src/components/BlockingViews/FullPageNotFoundView.tsx` — `StatsCounter` triggering Graphite calls ## Affected Scope - Only pending card transactions (posted transactions are not affected) - Observed on web (staging) - Likely affects all platforms ## Slack Reports - [Slack thread 1](https://expensify.slack.com/archives/C05LX9D6E07/p1773869694609889) (dbarrett - Mar 19) - [Slack thread 2](https://expensify.slack.com/archives/C05LX9D6E07/p1773393704658269) (earlier report) ## Platforms - [x] Web - [ ] iOS (likely affected) - [ ] Android (likely affected)
Issue OwnerCurrent Issue Owner: @situchan

  • Upwork Job ID: 2034603130719145071

title: [$250] [VBBA] connectBankAccountWithPlaid can send bankAccountID as NaN after verify-account resume flow

labels.name: Reviewing
labels.description: Has a PR in review

labels.name: Weekly
labels.description: KSv2

labels.name: Help Wanted
labels.description: Apply this label when an issue is open to proposals by contributors

labels.name: Bug
labels.description: Something is broken. Auto assigns a BugZero manager.

body:
## Problem In the USD reimbursement bank account flow, the `CONNECT_BANK_ACCOUNT_WITH_PLAID` request can be sent with `bankAccountID=NaN`. ## Reproduction 1. Use an account where `account.validated` is `false`. 2. Go to workspace bank account setup. 3. Choose **Connect online with Plaid**. 4. App redirects to verify account (`ROUTES.BANK_ACCOUNT_VERIFY_ACCOUNT.getRoute(policyID, backTo)`). 5. Complete validation and return to the reimbursement flow. 6. Complete Plaid auth, select a bank account (Savings/Checking), and continue (quick click on Next makes this easier to hit). 7. Inspect the API payload for `CONNECT_BANK_ACCOUNT_WITH_PLAID`: - `bankAccountID` is sent as `"NaN"`. ## Root Cause - The unvalidated path in `VerifiedBankAccountFlowEntryPoint.handleConnectPlaid()` returns early and navigates to verification. - After validation, flow resumes via `reimbursementAccountOptionPressed` state. - In this resumed path, `reimbursementAccount.achData.bankAccountID` can still be missing. - `BankInfo` computes: - `Number(reimbursementAccount?.achData?.bankAccountID)` - If source is `undefined`, result is `NaN`. - `connectBankAccountWithPlaid()` builds request params with this value, so payload can contain `NaN`. ## Impact - Frontend logic that relies on `bankAccountID === 0` (notably Chase manual fallback checks) can be bypassed when value is `NaN`. - Backend coercion currently masks this in some cases (`"NaN"` -> `0`), which hides the client-side correctness issue. ## Expected Behavior `bankAccountID` should never be `NaN` in client payloads. For new/setup flows, it should consistently be `0`. ## PR Scope - Normalize `bankAccountID` before request creation in `connectBankAccountWithPlaid` (non-finite -> `CONST.DEFAULT_NUMBER_ID`). - Ensure resumed post-validation flow initializes bank setup state consistently.
Issue OwnerCurrent Issue Owner: @ikevin127

labels.description: Added to denote the issue can be worked on by a contributor

labels.name: Daily
labels.description: KSv2

labels.name: Improvement
labels.description: Item broken or needs improvement.

labels.name: Help Wanted
labels.description: Apply this label when an issue is open to proposals by contributors

body:
Problem: Currently, when an admin of a workspace is searching for a category in the selector, and doesn't see one they need, they have to leave the expense and head to workspace settings > categories > add new category, then head back to the expense to select it. Solution: let's add the option for an **_admin whose workspace isn't connected to an accounting integration_** to add a new category when in the category list. Clicking it will allow the admin to create a new category there and then and apply it to the expense.x Mocks for reference:
